title: Ensure end-to-end TLS encryption is enabled

input_row.title: Ensure end-to-end TLS encryption is enabled
input_row.description: End-to-end (E2E) TLS encryption ensures that front-end to worker communication within function app deployment slots is encrypted using TLS. Without this feature, while incoming HTTPS requests are encrypted to the front ends, the traffic from front ends to workers running the application workloads would travel unencrypted inside Azure’s infrastructure. This recommendation applies to function apps using the Consumption, Premium, or Dedicated (App Service) plans, which support deployment slots.
input_row.rationale: E2E TLS helps ensure full encryption of traffic between: • Clients and front ends • Front ends and worker processes hosting the application
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list function apps: az functionapp list For each function app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az functionapp deployment slot list --resource-group -- name For each deployment slot, ensure that endToEndEncryptionEnabled is set to true. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: cbe0e5eb-fea9-491d-ab20-a62cf049c5ae - Name: 'Function app slots should enable end to end encryption'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to enable end-to-end TLS encryption: az resource update --resource-group --name /slots/ --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" --set properties.endToEndEncryptionEnabled=true
input_row.impact: Enabling end-to-end TLS encryption may introduce minimal latency and require additional configuration of certificates and HTTPS settings to ensure compatibility.
input_row.default_value: By default, end-to-end TLS encryption is disabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_lambda_function_deployment_slot_tls_encryption_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
To verify if end-to-end TLS encryption is enabled for function apps in AWS, the following automated checks will be performed:

1. **AWS API Calls**: Use the Boto3 SDK for AWS Lambda to check the configuration of function app deployment slots.
   - Use `boto3.client('lambda')` to create a client for AWS Lambda.
   - Enumerate all Lambda functions using `list_functions`.
   - For each function, check if there are any deployment slots. Unfortunately, AWS Lambda does not have deployment slots similar to Azure Function Apps, but you can check the properties of each function.
   
2. **AWS CLI Commands**: The similar check can be done via AWS CLI using:
   - `aws lambda list-functions` to list all Lambda functions in the account.

3. **Configuration Properties**: While AWS Lambda does not have a direct equivalent of the end-to-end TLS configuration found in Azure for deployment slots, the overall security posture can be assessed by checking:
   - Ensure that the function is configured with a VPC endpoint or an ALB (Application Load Balancer) that has SSL termination, thereby enforcing that communications going to these functions are encrypted.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
   - Pass: If the Lambda function has a VPC configured or an ALB pointing to it that is configured with HTTPS for communication.
   - Fail: If the function is accessible via HTTP or lacks appropriate SSL protections via an ALB.

This checks for best practices in encryption of communications to Lambda functions, acknowledging AWS's architectural differences from Azure.
